prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(enroll_account_for_sweep funding_account_operating)
(enroll_account_for_sweep receiving_account_buffer)
(enroll_account_for_sweep sweep_engine_primary)
(enroll_account_for_sweep sweep_engine_secondary)
(link_account_to_destination_profile funding_account_operating destination_profile_standard)
(associate_cash_snapshot_with_account funding_account_operating cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(confirm_account_eligibility funding_account_operating cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(apply_instruction_template_to_source_leg funding_account_operating sweep_leg_funding_1 sweep_template_standard)
(prioritize_source_leg funding_account_operating sweep_leg_funding_1 cash_snapshot_funding_1 sweep_template_standard)
(link_account_to_destination_profile receiving_account_buffer destination_profile_high)
(release_cash_snapshot funding_account_operating cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(associate_cash_snapshot_with_account receiving_account_buffer cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(confirm_account_eligibility receiving_account_buffer cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(apply_instruction_template_to_destination_leg receiving_account_buffer counterparty_leg_buffer_1 sweep_template_standard)
(prioritize_destination_leg receiving_account_buffer counterparty_leg_buffer_1 cash_snapshot_funding_1 sweep_template_standard)
(assemble_sweep_order_from_prioritized_legs funding_account_operating receiving_account_buffer sweep_leg_funding_1 counterparty_leg_buffer_1 sweep_order_batch_1)
(release_cash_snapshot receiving_account_buffer cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(associate_cash_snapshot_with_account funding_account_operating cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(confirm_order_settlement_ready sweep_order_batch_1 funding_account_operating cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(register_source_account_execution funding_account_operating sweep_order_batch_1)
(register_destination_account_execution receiving_account_buffer sweep_order_batch_1)
(link_account_to_destination_profile sweep_engine_primary destination_profile_low)
(release_cash_snapshot funding_account_operating cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(associate_cash_snapshot_with_account sweep_engine_primary cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(confirm_account_eligibility sweep_engine_primary cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(bind_settlement_instruction_to_order sweep_engine_primary settlement_instruction_ach sweep_order_batch_1)
(bind_instrument_to_engine sweep_engine_primary settlement_instruction_ach sweep_order_batch_1 cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(attach_buffer_policy_to_engine sweep_engine_primary buffer_policy_standard)
(attach_authorization_profile_to_account sweep_engine_primary auth_profile_standard)
(approve_engine_for_execution sweep_engine_primary buffer_policy_standard auth_profile_standard settlement_instruction_ach sweep_order_batch_1)
(attach_compliance_check_to_engine sweep_engine_primary compliance_check_limits)
(finalize_engine_approval_no_order_flags sweep_engine_primary compliance_check_limits settlement_instruction_ach sweep_order_batch_1)
(commit_and_register_engine_execution sweep_engine_primary)
(release_cash_snapshot sweep_engine_primary cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(associate_cash_snapshot_with_account funding_account_operating cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(rotate_timing_profile_and_tag_account_post_execution funding_account_operating timing_profile_eod cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(relink_source_account_for_next_sweep funding_account_operating destination_profile_standard timing_profile_eod)
(release_cash_snapshot funding_account_operating cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(associate_cash_snapshot_with_account receiving_account_buffer cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(rotate_timing_profile_and_tag_account_post_execution receiving_account_buffer timing_profile_eod cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(relink_destination_account_for_next_sweep receiving_account_buffer destination_profile_high timing_profile_eod)
(release_cash_snapshot receiving_account_buffer cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(associate_cash_snapshot_with_account sweep_engine_primary cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(rotate_timing_profile_and_tag_account_post_execution sweep_engine_primary timing_profile_eod cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(relink_engine_for_next_sweep sweep_engine_primary destination_profile_low timing_profile_eod)
(link_account_to_destination_profile sweep_engine_secondary destination_profile_standard)
(release_cash_snapshot sweep_engine_primary cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(associate_cash_snapshot_with_account sweep_engine_secondary cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(confirm_account_eligibility sweep_engine_secondary cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(bind_settlement_instruction_to_order sweep_engine_secondary settlement_instruction_swift sweep_order_batch_1)
(bind_instrument_to_engine sweep_engine_secondary settlement_instruction_swift sweep_order_batch_1 cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(detach_buffer_policy_from_engine sweep_engine_primary buffer_policy_standard)
(attach_buffer_policy_to_engine sweep_engine_secondary buffer_policy_standard)
(detach_compliance_check_from_engine sweep_engine_primary compliance_check_limits)
(attach_compliance_check_to_engine sweep_engine_secondary compliance_check_limits)
(detach_authorization_profile_from_account sweep_engine_primary auth_profile_standard)
(attach_authorization_profile_to_account sweep_engine_secondary auth_profile_standard)
(approve_engine_for_execution sweep_engine_secondary buffer_policy_standard auth_profile_standard settlement_instruction_swift sweep_order_batch_1)
(finalize_engine_approval_no_order_flags sweep_engine_secondary compliance_check_limits settlement_instruction_swift sweep_order_batch_1)
(commit_and_register_engine_execution sweep_engine_secondary)
(rotate_timing_profile_and_tag_account_post_execution sweep_engine_secondary timing_profile_eod cash_snapshot_funding_1)
(relink_engine_for_next_sweep sweep_engine_secondary destination_profile_standard timing_profile_eod)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for a treasury account sweep decisioning system. The goal is to get all four entities—funding_account_operating, receiving_account_buffer, sweep_engine_primary, and sweep_engine_secondary—relinked for next sweep, meaning (relinked_for_next_sweep ...) for each.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task is a planning problem using PDDL for a treasury account sweep decisioning system.  
- The goal is to ensure all four entities—funding_account_operating, receiving_account_buffer, sweep_engine_primary, and sweep_engine_secondary—are relinked for the next sweep.  

## Defining the Goal  
- Relinking means achieving the state (relinked_for_next_sweep ...) for each of the four entities.  
- The focus is on coordinating these entities to prepare for the upcoming sweep process.

## Extending the Planning Process  
- Initially, ofile_available is true for timing_profile_eod, setting the starting context for the sweep process.  
- Relinking requires execution_completed, account_destination_profile_link, and account_timing_profile_link, indicating a multi-step flow.  

## Analyzing Account Types  
- All entities—funding_account_operating, receiving_account_buffer, sweep_engine_primary, and sweep_engine_secondary—are subtypes of ledger_account, including source_account, destination_account, and sweep_engine.  
- The system uses two engines (primary with ACH, secondary with SWIFT), both linked to the same source, destination, and sweep_order_batch_1.  

## Managing Enrollment and Linking  
- First step is to enroll all four accounts using enroll_account_for_sweep, with preconditions of not enrolled and not relinked, initially true for all.  
- Then, link accounts to one of three destination profiles (high, standard, low) using link_account_to_destination_profile, requiring the account be enrolled and not already linked.  

## Handling Shared Resources  
- Only one cash_snapshot_funding_1 is available, but actions suggest associating it with specific accounts, possibly requiring release and reuse via release_cash_snapshot.  
- Similarly, only auth_profile_standard is available, likely requiring attach/detach actions to link it to multiple accounts as needed.

## Mapping Account Flows  
- Source accounts follow a typical flow: enroll, link to destination profile, associate cash snapshot, confirm eligibility, and attach authorization profile.  
- Two paths exist for source legs: select and reserve (prioritizing), or apply template then prioritize (selecting and prioritizing).  

## Examining Assembly Options  
- Order assembly has four versions based on prioritized or templated legs, requiring reserved sources and destinations, leg links, and selected legs.  
- Effects include assembled orders, leg links, and flags like source_template_flag, with orders transitioning from open to not open.  

## Managing Settlement Readiness  
- Confirming order settlement readiness requires an assembled order, reserved source, and linked cash, marking it as ready.  
- Binding settlement instructions to orders needs an eligible engine, engine links, and a ready, assembled order, marking it as bound.

## Reviewing Escalation Policies  
- Final gating requires approval completion, escalation request, execution mode link, and bound source/destination with prioritized status.  
- Post-gating commit needs both approval completion and gate passage, ensuring all prerequisites are met.  

## Exploring Escalation Availability  
- Only escalation_policy_primary is initially available, linked to sweep_engine_primary, while sweep_engine_secondary links to escalation_policy_secondary but it's not available.  
- Attaching an escalation policy to an engine requires the policy to be available, linked cash, and engine eligibility, affecting availability after attachment.  

## Planning Execution Sequence  
- The goal is to achieve relinked status for all four accounts, requiring execution completion for each.  
- This suggests a sequence involving entity execution, possibly following the established flows for enrollment and linking.

## Outlining Account Processing  
- Start by enrolling all four accounts to begin the process.  
- Focus on funding_account_operating first, linking it to destination_profile_standard for destination profile setup.  

## Associating Cash and Eligibility  
- Associate cash_snapshot_funding_1 with funding_account_operating to establish link_cash.  
- Confirm eligibility for funding_account_operating after linking cash, ensuring readiness for further actions.  

## Choosing Instruction Paths  
- Two options exist: apply a template or select and reserve legs, with template path not requiring authorization initially.  
- Opt for template path for funding_account_operating, using sweep_template_standard as it’s available and doesn’t need prior authorization.  

## Executing Template Workflow  
- Apply sweep_template_standard to sweep_leg_funding_1, reserving the account and applying the template, no longer available afterward.  
- Prioritize the leg with cash_snapshot_funding_1, marking it selected and prioritized, completing the funding leg setup.

## Continuing Template Workflow  
- After prioritizing, both source and destination legs remain template-applied, but assemble requires non-prioritized status, creating a mismatch.  
- Realized the prioritized assemble path fits better, requiring selected and prioritized legs, which matches the current state after template and prioritize steps.  

## Assembling Sweep Order  
- Successfully assembled the sweep order using assemble_sweep_order_from_prioritized_legs, linking both legs and marking the order as not open.  
- Confirmed prerequisites like reserved status, leg links, and prioritization were met, ensuring a smooth assembly.  

## Preparing for Settlement Confirmation  
- Noticed link_cash is currently associated with the receiving account, not the source, blocking settlement readiness confirmation.  
- Planned to release cash from receiving and re-associate with the source to proceed with settlement checks.

## Linking Engine Components
- Attached buffer_policy_standard to sweep_engine_primary, establishing engine_buffer_link.
- Realized authorization is needed, so linked auth_profile_standard to sweep_engine_primary for account_auth_link.

## Preparing Engine Approval
- Approved sweep_engine_primary for execution using buffer_policy_standard, auth_profile_standard, and settlement_instruction_ach, marking it ready_for_approval.
- Attached compliance_check_limits to sweep_engine_primary to meet engine_comp_link requirement for final approval.

## Finalizing Engine Execution
- Finalized approval for sweep_engine_primary without order flags, completing the process since no escalation was required.
- Committed and registered sweep_engine_primary execution, meeting prerequisites like approval_complete and no escalation_required.

## Completing Execution Workflow
- Noted execution_completed requires registered status, link_cash, and timing availability, all currently met for sweep_engine_primary.
- Planned to rotate timing profile and tag account post-execution, setting account_timing_link to eod and marking execution as completed.

## Evaluating Secondary Engine  
- Noted secondary swift engine is available but not yet registered, lacking dp and cash links.  
- Realized secondary engine needs processing after primary and accounts are completed due to resource constraints.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Identified all three dps (standard-funding, high-receiving, low-primary) are currently in use, leaving none for the secondary engine.  
- Planned to complete and relink one entity, like funding account, to free up a dp for secondary engine processing.  

## Preparing Account Rotation  
- Found funding account needs link_cash to rotate, currently held by primary engine.  
- Decided to release cash from primary and associate it with funding account for rotation, consuming available timing.  

## Planning Execution Completion  
- Confirmed rotating funding account involves setting account_timing_profile_link to eod and releasing dp.  
- Realized primary engine also needs rotation later, requiring careful sequencing to manage shared resources.

## Linking Secondary Engine  
- Linked cash snapshot (cash_snapshot_funding_1) to sweep_engine_secondary, confirming eligibility for processing.  
- Bound settlement_instruction_swift to sweep_engine_secondary for sweep_order_batch_1, ensuring it’s available and not previously assigned.  

## Configuring Secondary Resources  
- Detached buffer_policy_standard, compliance_check_limits, and auth_profile_standard from sweep_engine_primary, making them available.  
- Attached the released resources (buffer, compliance, and auth) to sweep_engine_secondary, completing necessary links.  

## Approving Secondary Execution  
- Approved sweep_engine_secondary for execution with no flags, ensuring instrument_bound and all links (buffer, auth, settle) are in place.  
- Finalized approval without order flags, marking it approval_complete after meeting all prerequisites.  

## Registering Secondary Engine  
- Committed and registered sweep_engine_secondary execution, confirming approval_complete and no escalation required.  
- Noted cash remains linked to secondary, with timing still available from previous relink, setting up for potential rotation.

## Configuring Primary Engine  
- Attached buffer_policy_standard and auth_profile_standard to sweep_engine_primary, preparing for execution.  
- Bound settlement_instruction_ach to sweep_engine_primary for sweep_order_batch_1, ensuring alignment with order processing.  

## Approving Primary Execution  
- Approved sweep_engine_primary for execution, confirming all necessary links (buffer, auth, settle) are in place.  
- Finalized approval without order flags, marking it approval_complete after attaching compliance_check_limits.  

## Registering Primary Engine  
- Committed and registered sweep_engine_primary execution, noting cash remains linked to primary.  
- Identified need to release cash from primary for funding account rotation, setting up next steps.  

## Rotating Funding Account  
- Released cash_snapshot_funding_1 from sweep_engine_primary and associated it with funding_account_operating.  
- Rotated funding_account_operating to timing_profile_eod, relinking it for the next sweep with destination_profile_standard.  

## Rotating Receiving Account  
- Released cash_snapshot_funding_1 from funding_account_operating and associated it with receiving_account_buffer.  
- Rotated receiving_account_buffer to timing_profile_eod, relinking it for the next sweep with destination_profile_high.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
